name: Can I sue someone if I got hurt at work on a construction site in Ogden?
description: Workers' comp from employer; sue third parties like subs or owners if negligent.

mainEntity.name: Can I sue someone if I got hurt at work on a construction site in Ogden?

acceptedAnswer.text: Workers' comp from employer; sue third parties like subs or owners if negligent.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Yes, you can pursue workers' compensation from your employer and potentially sue third parties like negligent subcontractors or equipment makers in Utah.** Workers' comp is usually the exclusive remedy against your employer, but third-party claims allow extra recovery for negligence[2][3]. **Key factors:** 1. Prove third-party duty, breach, and causation. 2. Common liable parties: general contractors, property owners, manufacturers[1][4]. 3. Utah's 50% comparative fault rule applies—if you're less than 50% at fault, recover reduced damages[1]. 4. File within 4 years[3]. Document everything and hire a lawyer to identify all liable parties. This can cover pain, suffering, and full losses workers' comp misses[4].
